title: Training Module – Flow Cytometry
summary: Conduct cell cycle analysis using flow cytometry to determine the percentage of cells in G0/G1, S, and G2/M phases for specific somatic cell lines.

key_factors.why: The experiment requires analyzing cell cycle distributions specifically for these three cell lines.
PI binds to DNA to provide a fluorescent signal proportional to DNA content, which is required to differentiate cell cycle phases.
These specific cell cycle gates are required to calculate the percentage distribution of the cell population.

sequence.step: Select somatic cell line (FL1, FL2, or FL3)
sequence.why: Establishes the target biological sample for analysis.
Labels the DNA to allow quantification of DNA content via fluorescence.

sequence.step: Run the sample on the flow cytometer
sequence.why: Measures the fluorescence signal for individual cells in the suspension.

sequence.step: Perform cell cycle analysis using gating
sequence.why: Calculates the percentage of the population in G0/G1, S, and G2/M phases based on DNA content.
